paths.notes: This file itself. Only writable by `code-architect owners bootstrap` or `owners migrate`; never a general edit target.
Config dir for CA-managed lockfiles + contracts. Run.lock is handled atomically by safe-json; humans review anything else.

Per-client engagement dossiers (proposals, SOWs, services agreements, follow-up email drafts, scope-reset emails, pricing decisions). Each subdir contains client PII: contact names, contract terms, dollar figures, rate-card decisions. OA owns the client folder lifecycle and writes these as part of its pricing/negotiation workflow. CA must never touch — both to keep client financials out of CA's context window and because creative/economic ownership belongs to OA + Alex, not the engineering agent.
OA's mesh inbox for pitch/brief work orders from KMG/Kai. Runtime state; OA's poller writes. CA must never touch.
OA's mesh outbox for outbound pitch/brief sends. Runtime state; OA writes. CA must never touch.
Shared mesh state plus cross-agent review drops (e.g., oa-review-*.md from OA-as-reviewer). Owned by OA's runtime + filesystem-drop mailman pattern; CA never writes.
Daemon-mode API inventory (Brave Search, 10 Google Cloud APIs). Tied to credentials + cost ceilings; humans review.
Authoritative pricing floors + Go/No-Go decision logic. Load-bearing for every offer OA produces; humans review every change.
Negotiation strategy playbook. Strategic content; humans review.
SOW + contract templates and clauses. Legal/enforceability surface; humans review.
Agency capabilities statement. Tied to what OA may quote against; humans review.
Cross-client history ledger. Contains client names + outcomes (won/lost/lessons); OA owns the entries. Treat as PII-adjacent; CA never writes.
Pattern library distilled from past engagements. OA writes from its own learning loop; CA never writes.
Worked offer examples. May reference real client figures; humans review additions.
Research audit methodology + output schema (used by daemon-mode). Schema changes affect downstream consumers; humans review.
Notes on research tooling. Humans review.
Core pricing + offer-evaluation skill (SKILL.md + assets). Behavior-critical for every pricing decision OA emits; humans review.
Research + discovery skill (also drives autonomous research audits in daemon mode). Behavior-critical; humans review.
Session shutdown skill — preserves OA session state. Humans review behavior changes to OA's memory protocol.
Catch-all for any future skills under .claude/skills/. Default to human review until a sub-lane is carved out explicitly.
Catch-all for .claude/ config (settings.json, hooks, permissions, etc.). Behavior-shaping; humans review.
CI/CD workflows (deploy.yml present). Deploy-pipeline; CLAUDE.md T3 + DA-mandatory.
Gitignore rules — controls what client binaries (PDFs/HTML/full-site dirs) are excluded from version control. Loosening risks leaking client assets; humans review.
OA agent definition: identity, startup protocol, skills wiring, operating modes, hard rules, style. Behavior root; humans review every change.

fallback.notes: Default-deny posture. This repo has no scripts/, tests/, lib/, src/, or any code tree as of bootstrap — it is a docs+skills agent. Any future code additions (poller, daemon, helpers) land here under human review until a sub-lane is carved out explicitly. No auto_merge lanes exist in this bootstrap: there is no library or test code that would benefit from mechanical-refactor bypass yet.
